name: backfill_quarter_scores.py
summary: Backfills missing quarter scores (q1–q4) for existing game records by fetching line scores from CFBD, with dry-run and batch commit support.

name: test_cfbd_data.py

name: update_games.py

name: compare_rankings.py

name: import_player_data.py

name: backfill_predictions.py

name: generate_predictions.py

name: generate_ranking_comparison_report.py

summary: Core domain logic implementing the Modified ELO ranking algorithm with preseason factors, progressive K-factor, garbage time detection, SOS calculation, prediction generation, and accuracy tracking.

name: reprocess_season.py

name: save_final_season_snapshot.py

name: main.py

name: clear-cache.sh
summary: VPS maintenance script to clear Python bytecode cache (.pyc files and __pycache__ dirs) when code changes aren't being picked up. Stops the cfb-rankings systemd service, removes all .pyc files and __pycache__ directories under /var/www/cfb-rankings, then restarts the service and shows status.

name: deploy-epic-004.sh
summary: EPIC-004 deployment script for the VPS. Pulls latest code from git, verifies CFBD_MONTHLY_LIMIT is present in .env (prompting to add if missing), installs Python dependencies via pip, runs a Python heredoc to apply database schema updates, then restarts the service. Includes colored terminal output for step visibility.

name: deploy-epic-009.sh
summary: EPIC-009 deployment script for Prediction Accuracy Tracking & Display (v3.0.0, LOW risk, no database migration). Pulls latest code, verifies commits, restarts the cfb-rankings service, then smoke-tests three endpoints: /api/predictions/accuracy, /api/predictions/accuracy/team/82, and /api/predictions/stored.

name: deploy.sh
summary: Main deployment script (requires root). Pulls latest code from git origin/main, installs requirements-prod.txt into the venv, restarts the cfb-rankings systemd service, and reloads nginx (with config test). App lives at /var/www/cfb-rankings. Database migration step is commented out for regular deployments.

name: fix-permissions.sh
summary: File ownership and permission fix script for EPIC-004 deployment. Sets www-data:www-data ownership recursively on /var/www/cfb-rankings, database file to 664, backup files, and .env file to 640. Intended to be run after initial deploy or when permission errors occur.

name: setup.sh
summary: One-time VPS initial setup script (requires root). Installs system packages (python3, nginx, certbot, git), creates /var/www/cfb-rankings, clones or accepts manually placed app files, creates Python venv, installs requirements-prod.txt, creates log directories, copies .env.example to .env and opens nano for editing, initializes the SQLite database, optionally runs import_real_data.py, sets www-data ownership, installs deploy/cfb-rankings.service to systemd, configures nginx from deploy/nginx.conf (substituting domain), and obtains a Let's Encrypt SSL certificate via certbot.

name: cfb-rankings.service
summary: Systemd service unit for the main FastAPI application. Runs as www-data, WorkingDirectory /var/www/cfb-rankings, ExecStart uses gunicorn with gunicorn_config.py and main:app. Type=simple, Restart=on-failure with 10s RestartSec. Sets PATH to venv/bin, DATABASE_URL, and CFBD_API_KEY via inline Environment directives (note: key is hardcoded in this file). WantedBy multi-user.target.

name: cfb-weekly-update.service
summary: Systemd oneshot service unit for the weekly data import job. Runs as www-data, loads env from /var/www/cfb-rankings/.env, executes scripts/weekly_update.py via venv Python. Logs append to /var/log/cfb-rankings/weekly-update.log. 30-minute timeout, Restart=no (timer handles scheduling). Security-hardened: NoNewPrivileges, PrivateTmp, ProtectSystem=strict, ProtectHome, ReadWritePaths limited to app and log dirs.

name: cfb-weekly-update.timer
summary: Systemd timer that triggers cfb-weekly-update.service every Sunday at 22:00 UTC (5:00 PM Eastern during EST, 6:00 PM Eastern during EDT). Persistent=true ensures missed runs execute on next boot. RandomizedDelaySec=300 adds up to 5 minutes of jitter for network stability. WantedBy timers.target.

name: nginx.conf
summary: Nginx site configuration for college football rankings. HTTP (port 80) redirects to HTTPS. HTTPS (port 443) with TLS 1.2/1.3 via Let's Encrypt certificates: serves frontend static files from /var/www/cfb-rankings/frontend with 1-year cache headers for assets, and reverse-proxies /api/, /docs, /redoc, /openapi.json to Gunicorn at 127.0.0.1:8000 with 60s timeouts. Security headers: X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, X-XSS-Protection.

name: 26.8.story.md
summary: EPIC-026 Story 26.8 (Completed 2026-05-01). Validates star-based transfer portal team rankings against 247Sports expert rankings for 2024 season. Spearman rank correlation of ~0.52 (moderate alignment). Key finding: our algorithm is quantity-focused (rewards transfer volume), 247Sports is quality-focused (rewards 4★/5★ transfers). Decision made to retain current algorithm due to volume's real ELO impact and 88.7% star data coverage. Comparison utility at utilities/compare_transfer_rankings.py.

name: 28.1.story.md
summary: EPIC-028 Story 28.1 (Complete). Create CONTRIBUTING.md and DEVELOPMENT.md developer onboarding documentation covering local setup, environment configuration, test running, and contribution workflow. Zero-risk documentation-only change with no code modifications.

name: 28.2.story.md
summary: EPIC-028 Story 28.2 (Complete). Create CODING-STANDARDS.md documentation, add pyproject.toml with Black and isort configuration (line-length=88, profile=black), create .flake8 config, and add requirements-dev.txt with development tooling. Establishes code quality toolchain configuration.

name: 28.3.story.md
summary: EPIC-028 Story 28.3 (Complete). Add comprehensive Google-style docstrings to all core modules: main.py (20+ endpoints), ranking_service.py (15+ methods), models.py (10+ classes), schemas.py (8+ schemas), database.py (3+ functions), cfbd_client.py (8+ methods). Enables auto-generated OpenAPI documentation and IDE support.

name: 28.4.story.md
summary: EPIC-028 Story 28.4 (Complete). Run isort on all Python files to standardize import statement ordering: 54 root-level .py files, 20 files in scripts/, and the tests/ directory. Uses isort with profile=black configuration for compatibility with Black formatter.

name: 28.5.story.md
summary: EPIC-028 Story 28.5 (Complete). Reorganize project files by moving migrate_add_*.py scripts to migrations/ directory, and moving check_*.py, debug_*.py, diagnose_*.py scripts to diagnostics/ directory using git mv to preserve history. Improves project structure and separates concerns.

name: 28.6.story.md
summary: EPIC-028 Story 28.6 (Complete, Medium-High risk). Create src/ package structure with api/, core/, models/, integrations/ subdirectories and move core files via git mv: main.py → src/api/, ranking_service.py → src/core/, models.py+schemas.py+database.py → src/models/, cfbd_client.py → src/integrations/. Update all import statements across the codebase to use src. prefix. Most significant refactor in the epic.

name: 28.7.story.md
summary: EPIC-028 Story 28.7 (Complete). Archive one-off fix_*.py scripts to archive/ directory, move remaining utility scripts to utilities/, apply Black formatter to all Python files, and generate Flake8 lint report documenting remaining violations. Completes the codebase organization and formatting epic.

name: EPIC-003-DEV-SUMMARY.md
summary: EPIC-003 development summary for FCS game display feature. Documents completed implementation: FCS games stored with excluded_from_rankings=True flag, displayed on team schedules with gray/muted visual styling, opponent shown as team name or 'FCS Opponent'. Covers all 3 stories (model flag, API import, frontend display) and confirms zero regressions on existing FBS game rankings.

name: epic-003-fcs-game-display.md
summary: EPIC-003 definition document for FCS game display. Defines the problem (FCS games excluded from rankings but not visible to users), solution (store with excluded_from_rankings=True, display with visual distinction), and 3 stories: add Game model exclusion flag, import FCS games via CFBD API, and display FCS games on team schedules with grayed-out styling.

name: preseason-1.1-player-model.md
summary: Preseason Epic Story 1.1 (Completed). Create Player SQLAlchemy model in src/models/models.py with fields: cfbd_athlete_id, name, team_id (FK), position, stars, rating, ranking, recruiting_year, created_at. Generate and run Alembic migration to add players table. Foundation for position strength preseason calculations.

name: preseason-1.2-cfbd-player-api.md
summary: Preseason Epic Story 1.2 (Completed). Add get_recruiting_players() method to CFBDClient in src/integrations/cfbd_client.py, calling the CFBD /recruiting/players endpoint with year and team parameters. Returns list of player dicts with star ratings and position data for importing into the Player model.

name: preseason-1.3-position-service.md
summary: Preseason Epic Story 1.3 (Completed). Create src/core/position_service.py with calculate_position_strength() function and position_weights.json config file. Weights: QB=0.30, OL=0.25, DL=0.20, DB=0.15, LB=0.05, RB=0.025, WR=0.025, TE=0.0, ST=0.0. Position scores normalized 0-100, bonus capped at max_bonus. Feature flag enabled=false by default.

name: preseason-1.4-player-import.md
summary: Preseason Epic Story 1.4 (Completed). Create utilities/import_player_data.py script with CLI flags --year, --team, --dry-run, --force. Fetches recruiting player data from CFBD API and inserts into Player table, respecting existing records (skip or overwrite with --force). Supports importing one team or all FBS teams for a given year.

name: preseason-1.5-api-endpoints.md
summary: Preseason Epic Story 1.5 (Completed). Add two FastAPI endpoints to src/api/main.py: GET /api/teams/{team_id}/players (filtering by recruiting_year, position, pagination via skip/limit, sorted by rating desc) and GET /api/teams/{team_id}/position-strength (returns enabled flag, position_scores, position_bonus, max_bonus, weights, recruiting_year). 18 integration tests created.

name: preseason-1.6-integration.md
summary: Preseason Epic Story 1.6 (Completed). Integrate position strength bonus into calculate_preseason_rating() in src/core/ranking_service.py via new _calculate_position_strength_bonus() helper. Feature flag (config['enabled']) gates the bonus; graceful degradation returns 0.0 on disabled/error/missing data. 10 unit tests cover all scenarios. Formula: base + recruiting + transfer + returning + position_strength_bonus.

name: story-001-games-page-display.md
summary: EPIC-001 Story 1 (Complete). Update games.html to display all season games including future/scheduled games. Fetches from /api/games endpoint and renders both completed (with scores) and upcoming (with date/time, TBD score) games. Covers full season game list view.

name: story-002-team-detail-schedule.md
summary: EPIC-001 Story 2 (Complete). Update team detail page to show full season schedule including future games. Team schedule section fetches from /api/teams/{id}/schedule and renders upcoming games with opponent, date, and predicted outcome alongside completed games with results.

name: story-003-visual-enhancements.md
summary: EPIC-001 Story 3 (Complete). Add visual styling to distinguish completed vs scheduled games across the app: completed games show W/L outcome badges with green/red styling, scheduled games show a clock icon and muted styling. CSS additions to frontend styles.

name: story-004-dynamic-season-detection-utility.md
summary: EPIC-002 Story 4 (Complete). Create utility functions to auto-detect the current season year and current week from the CFBD API, replacing hardcoded values in import scripts. Implements get_current_season() and get_current_week() in cfbd_client.py using CFBD /games endpoint with status=completed parameter.

name: story-005-remove-hardcoded-seasons.md
summary: EPIC-002 Story 5 (Complete). Remove all hardcoded year values (e.g., 2024) from import scripts and replace with dynamic detection using get_current_season(). Affects weekly_update.py, import scripts, and admin trigger endpoints. Eliminates manual year updates between seasons.

name: story-006-import-validation-completeness.md
summary: EPIC-002 Story 6 (Complete). Add import validation to check completeness after each weekly import: compare expected game count from CFBD API vs actual imported count, log warnings for missing games, and include completeness report in import output. Helps detect partial import failures.

name: story-007-game-model-exclusion-flag.md
summary: EPIC-003 Story 7 (Complete). Add excluded_from_rankings Boolean field (default False) to the Game SQLAlchemy model in src/models/models.py and create corresponding Alembic migration. Enables marking FCS games and other non-ranking games without deleting them from the database.

name: story-008-fcs-game-import-api.md
summary: EPIC-003 Story 8 (Complete). Update game import logic in cfbd_client.py to fetch FCS opponent games (where opponent is not an FBS team) and set excluded_from_rankings=True. Update /api/teams/{id}/schedule endpoint to include FCS games in response with is_fcs_opponent flag.

name: story-009-fcs-frontend-display.md
summary: EPIC-003 Story 9 (Complete). Display FCS games on team schedule pages with visual distinction: grayed-out/muted styling, 'FCS' label badge, opponent shown as team name or 'FCS Opponent'. Uses CSS .fcs-game class for styling, frontend-only change to team detail JavaScript rendering.

name: story-ap-comparison-data-fix.md
summary: Fix story for AP comparison page showing zero values. Root cause: AP poll data was not being imported during weekly updates. Fix imports AP poll rankings from CFBD /rankings endpoint alongside game data, stores in APPoll table, and comparison page correctly displays ELO vs AP Poll accuracy charts.

name: story-cfbd-01-investigate-api-schema.md
summary: Investigation story to examine the CFBD API response schema and document actual field naming conventions. Found that CFBD uses camelCase in JSON responses (e.g., homePoints, awayPoints, startDate) rather than snake_case assumed in early code. Produces schema reference document for use in cfbd_client.py and test mock updates.

name: story-cfbd-02-fix-test-mocks.md
summary: Fix story to update test mocks in the test suite to match actual CFBD API camelCase response schema (homePoints, awayPoints, startDate, etc.) discovered in story-cfbd-01. Corrects test mock data so unit tests accurately reflect real API responses and prevent false-passing tests.

name: story-fix-admin-endpoint-imports.md
summary: Fix story (Complete). Resolve ModuleNotFoundError in admin endpoints by correcting 3 import paths in src/api/main.py from bare 'from cfbd_client import ...' to 'from src.integrations.cfbd_client import ...'. Part of post-EPIC-028 src/ reorganization cleanup. Fixes 5 integration tests that were failing due to import errors.

name: story-fix-admin-updatetask-import.md
summary: Fix story for UnboundLocalError in trigger_update admin endpoint caused by UpdateTask class not being imported before use in exception handler. Fix imports UpdateTask at the top of the function or module scope. Resolves 4 failing unit tests in the admin endpoint test suite.

name: story-fix-cfbd-current-week-detection.md
summary: Fix story for CFBDClient.get_current_week() returning None instead of the correct week number. Root cause: query logic did not handle the case where all weeks had completed games, returning None instead of the max week. Fix adds fallback to return max completed week when no in-progress week is found. Resolves 2 failing unit tests.

name: story-fix-comparison-page-error.md
summary: Fix story for comparison page returning HTTP 500 when AP Poll data is unavailable. Replace hard failure with graceful error handling: return empty datasets with an informative message rather than crashing. Frontend displays 'AP Poll data not available' state instead of blank/broken chart.

name: story-fix-game-import-tests.md
summary: Fix story (Complete, ~30min). Resolve game import test assertion failures caused by mismatched expected vs actual field values in test fixtures after CFBD schema corrections. Updates test assertions and mock data to match corrected camelCase-to-snake_case field mappings in cfbd_client.py.

name: story-fix-integration-test-imports.md
summary: Fix story (Closed, not reproducible). Investigation into integration test import failures that could not be reproduced in a clean environment. Documents the investigation steps taken and confirms the issue was transient or environment-specific. No code changes made.

name: story-fix-rankings-teams-test-data.md
summary: Fix story (Complete). Update rankings and teams API test fixtures to create RankingHistory records instead of only Team objects, reflecting the post-EPIC-024 architecture change where rankings are stored in RankingHistory table rather than on Team directly. Also creates proper Game records in test setup. Fixes 8 failing integration tests.

name: story-fix-season-year-detection.md
summary: Fix story for CFBDClient.get_current_season() incorrectly advancing to the next season year during January playoff period. Fix adds date-range logic: if current date is in January and playoff games are still in progress, stay on the prior year's season rather than jumping to the new year. Ensures correct data imports during bowl/playoff season.

name: story-fix-testclient-fixture.md
summary: Fix story (Complete, ~1hr). Resolve TestClient fixture initialization error in integration tests caused by FastAPI app startup event failing during test setup. Fix configures test database override and ensures lifespan context is properly mocked or bypassed in test fixtures.

name: story-fix-weekly-update-exit-calls.md
summary: Fix story for weekly_update.py calling sys.exit() multiple times per error scenario (once in the error handler and again in the finally block), causing confusing exit codes. Refactor to call sys.exit() exactly once per error path, typically at the end of the main function. Fixes 3 failing unit tests.

name: story-game-dates-01-ui-display.md
summary: EPIC-GAME-DATE-SORTING Story 1. Add game date column to games.html table, displaying formatted date (e.g., 'Jan 1') for each game. Frontend-only change using existing game_date field already returned by /api/games endpoint. Especially useful for bowl and playoff games that span multiple days in the same week.

name: story-game-dates-02-api-sorting.md
summary: EPIC-GAME-DATE-SORTING Story 2. Update /api/games and /api/teams/{id}/schedule endpoints to sort results by week first, then by game_date as a secondary sort (null dates sorted last). SQLAlchemy query change using ORDER BY week ASC, game_date ASC NULLS LAST for consistent chronological ordering.

name: story-game-dates-03-consistency.md
summary: EPIC-GAME-DATE-SORTING Story 3. Ensure consistent date display format across all views (games list, team schedule, predictions). Add relative date display for upcoming games (e.g., 'in 3 days', 'Tomorrow') to team schedule and prediction pages. Standardizes date presentation throughout the frontend.

name: story-import-playoff-predictions.md
summary: Story to import completed playoff game results and generate ELO-based predictions for the next playoff round. After each playoff round completes, runs import to update ELO ratings, then generates predictions for upcoming matchups. Supports the 12-team CFP bracket with first-round through championship predictions.

name: story-multi-year-comparison.md
summary: Story to add multi-year season selector to the AP Poll comparison page, allowing users to view ELO vs AP Poll accuracy for any historical season (not just the current one). Adds year dropdown to comparison.html, updates /api/comparison endpoint to accept optional year query parameter, defaults to current season.

name: story-playoff-01-diagnose-import-issues.md
summary: EPIC-FIX-PLAYOFF-GAMES Story 1. Diagnose why 2024 CFP playoff games have 0-0 placeholder scores or are missing entirely. Investigation reveals: week validation capped at 15 (excluded weeks 16-19), API call limit of 1,000 was hit before playoff games, and season_type='postseason' not used for first-round games. Documents root causes for stories 2 and 3.

name: story-playoff-02-import-fix-2024-games.md
summary: EPIC-FIX-PLAYOFF-GAMES Story 2. Import all 8 2024 CFP playoff games (4 first-round + 4 quarterfinals) with correct scores, dates, and ELO updates. Extends week validation from 0-15 to 0-19, increases API limit to 30,000, and handles TBD opponent placeholders for future rounds. One-time data fix for 2024 season.

name: story-playoff-03-sustainable-playoff-handling.md
summary: EPIC-FIX-PLAYOFF-GAMES Story 3. Enhance cfbd_client.py and weekly_update.py to automatically handle postseason games in future seasons: detect weeks 16-19 as postseason, use season_type='postseason' for API calls, skip TBD-opponent games until results are available. Ensures playoff imports work without manual intervention in future years.

name: story-season-end-date-config.md
summary: Story to add SEASON_END_DATE environment variable configuration for controlling when the system considers a season complete. Allows operators to set an explicit end date (e.g., January 20 for CFP championship) so that season detection logic stays on the correct year through the full playoff period.

name: story-verify-season-detection-consistency.md
summary: Verification story to audit and confirm that all season detection logic across the app (cfbd_client.py, weekly_update.py, admin endpoints, import scripts) is consistent and uses the same source of truth for current season/week. Documents any discrepancies found and ensures a single authoritative detection method is used everywhere.

name: README.md
summary: Frontend documentation covering the seven pages (rankings, team detail, all teams, games, prediction comparison, simulator, matchup), CSS architecture (tokens → base → layout → components), JavaScript module roles, and instructions for running the local dev server against the FastAPI backend on port 8000.

name: admin.html
summary: Password-gated admin dashboard that authenticates via X-Admin-Key header, then shows the last import run status, a rank-movement table for teams that moved 5+ spots, cron schedule health (Monday 9am UTC), and environment variable configuration reference (CFBD_API_KEY, ADMIN_SECRET, SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL, NOTIFY_EMAIL). Provides a 'Run Import Now' button that POSTs to /api/admin/import/results.

name: comparison.html
summary: Prediction accuracy comparison page that benchmarks ELO predictions against AP Poll picks using Chart.js visualizations and week-by-week accuracy statistics, demonstrating the system's predictive edge over traditional rankings.

name: elo-formula.html
summary: Interactive explainer page (769 lines) documenting the modified ELO algorithm with formula cards covering expected score, K-factor scaling, margin-of-victory multiplier, home-field advantage, preseason adjustments (recruiting, transfers, returning production, position strength), and strength-of-schedule calculation with live interactive sliders.

name: games.html
summary: Game schedule and results listing page with week-based filtering, displaying matchup outcomes, scores, ELO rating changes per game, and neutral-site indicators. Includes responsive CSS to hide or abbreviate the date column on small screens.

name: index.html
summary: Main rankings page and application entry point. Displays a stats grid (current week, total teams, games played, last updated), a top-25 ELO rankings table with rank-change badges, conference indicators (P5/G5/FCS), SOS difficulty color coding, and a game-predictions section. Supports season selector for historical seasons, week-by-week prediction simulation against actual results, a 'Share Top 25' canvas card download, and OG/Twitter social meta tags.

name: matchup.html
summary: Head-to-head matchup predictor that lets users search for any two college football teams and compare them side-by-side with ELO history charts, season stats, and win probability calculations derived from the ELO rating difference.

name: simulator.html
summary: Preseason rating simulator page with real-time slider controls for formula weight adjustments (recruiting, transfer portal, returning production, position strength) and previous-season regression parameters (prev_season_weight, mean_regression, returning_regression_scale). Renders a live simulated rankings table showing delta from official ratings; admins can persist slider values as the new official preseason configuration.

name: team.html
summary: Individual team detail page loaded via query parameter (?id=). Displays the team's current ELO rating and national rank, win-loss record, preseason component breakdown (recruiting, transfers, returning production), full game-by-game schedule with results and ELO change per game, and clickable opponent links.

name: teams.html
summary: Browseable listing of all FBS college football teams with their current ELO ratings, win-loss records, conference membership, and strength of schedule. Supports filtering by conference and sorting by ELO rating, with each row linking to the team detail page.

name: base.css
summary: CSS reset and base typography layer. Imports Inter from Google Fonts, applies universal box-sizing reset, sets body background and font from design tokens, defines heading scale (h1-h3), anchor hover transitions to accent gold, and utility classes (.hidden, .tabular, .text-center, margin helpers).

name: components.css
summary: Largest stylesheet (2074 lines) containing all UI component styles: rankings table with rank badges (top-5/top-10/top-25), conference badges (P5/G5/FCS), SOS difficulty indicators, prediction cards, ELO sparklines, stat cards, accuracy banner, historical season banner, admin dashboard components (status badges, stat rows, env table), simulator slider groups, matchup comparison panels, team profile stats, share-card button, loading spinner, error states, empty states, and responsive table overrides.

name: layout.css
summary: Structural layout styles for the site shell: sticky site header (60px, z-index 100), sticky secondary navigation bar (48px, z-index 99) with hamburger menu for mobile breakpoint (≤767px), 1280px max-width main container, and site footer with centered flex layout. Includes theme-toggle button styles and nav-link active/hover states.

name: style.css
summary: CSS entry point (9 lines) that stitches together the design system via four ordered @import statements: tokens.css → base.css → layout.css → components.css. All HTML pages link only this file.

name: tokens.css
summary: Design token definitions (113 lines) with dark-first theming. Defines CSS custom properties for surfaces (bg-primary through bg-card), border colors, text hierarchy (primary/secondary/muted), accent palette (gold #d69e2e with hover, dim, and blue secondary), semantic colors (success, warning, error, info), navigation height, font stacks (Inter sans-serif and JetBrains Mono), spacing radii (sm through xl), shadows (four levels), and transition speeds. Overrides for light theme via [data-theme='light'] and system prefers-color-scheme media query.

name: README.md
summary: Documentation for the utilities directory, describing its purpose as a collection of reusable helper scripts (distinct from one-off archive scripts). Catalogs development tools (seed_data.py, demo.py), analysis tools (compare_rankings.py), and the weekly update and preseason shell scripts, with usage examples for each.

name: finalize_2026_preseason.sh
summary: EPIC-029 Story 29.4 shell script that finalizes the 2026 preseason setup on the VPS. Verifies player data exists in the database, creates the 2026 Season record via scripts/start_new_season.py, and initializes preseason ELO ratings with full position-strength weighting. Intended to run after setup_2026_preseason.sh and the player data imports are complete.

name: setup_2026_preseason.sh
summary: EPIC-029 first-stage preseason setup script. Creates a database backup, reprocesses the 2025 season to fix ELO imbalances via utilities/reprocess_season.py, and archives the 2024 season record (sets is_active=False). Prints next-step instructions for running the multi-year player data imports before finalize_2026_preseason.sh.

name: weekly_update.sh
summary: Primary weekly automation script (379 lines, EPIC-033/035) run via cron every Monday at 9am UTC. Orchestrates four steps: (1) imports CFBD schedule and game results with up to 3 retry attempts and exponential backoff; (2) processes all unprocessed games through the ELO RankingService and saves weekly ranking_history snapshots; (3) computes a rank-movement diff report (teams that moved 5+ spots) and writes it to data/import_log.json; (4) restarts the cfb-rankings systemd service (skippable via SKIP_SERVICE_RESTART=1 when called from the API). Sends Slack webhook and/or email notifications on success or failure. Updates import_log.json with run metadata.

name: .flake8
summary: Flake8 linting configuration: max line length 100, ignores E203/W503 (black-compatible), excludes venv/migrations/archive/build directories, and suppresses F401 unused-import warnings in __init__.py files.

name: .coveragerc
summary: Pytest coverage configuration: measures branch coverage for the full source tree, omits tests/venv/seed_data/demo/compare_rankings/import_real_data/deploy from reports, outputs HTML to htmlcov/, and excludes defensive/abstract/type-checking lines from coverage counts.

name: pyproject.toml
summary: Python project tooling configuration: Black formatter (line-length 100, target py311) and isort import sorter (black profile, line-length 100, known_first_party=["src"]) with shared exclude lists covering migrations, archive, and venv directories.

name: launch.json
summary: Claude Code launch configuration with two run configurations: FastAPI dev server (uvicorn via arch -x86_64 on port 8000 with --reload) and a static frontend server (Python http.server on port 3000 serving the frontend/ directory).

name: bmad-doc-template.md
summary: BMad document template specification: defines YAML-based template format for interactive document generation with sections, variable substitution ({{variable}}), conditional blocks, repeatable sections, agent permission fields (owner/editors/readonly), choice selectors, and Mermaid diagram support. Includes processing flow, best practices, and migration guide from legacy markdown+frontmatter templates.

name: workflow-management.md
summary: BMad workflow management utility: enables the orchestrator to list, start, resume, and track multi-agent workflows. Defines commands (/workflows, /workflow-start, /workflow-status, /workflow-resume, /workflow-next), execution flow (stage transitions, artifact tracking, context passing), and multi-path conditional workflow handling.

name: .gitkeep
summary: Empty placeholder file that ensures the data/ directory is tracked by git even when it contains no other committed files.

name: api.js
summary: ApiService class providing a singleton REST client for all backend endpoints. Auto-detects localhost vs production base URL. Methods cover rankings (getRankings, getRankingHistory, saveRankings), teams (getTeams, getTeam, getTeamSchedule), games, seasons (getSeasons, getActiveSeason), predictions (getPredictions, getPredictionAccuracy, getStoredPredictions, getPredictionComparison), system stats, and ELO recalculation.

name: app.js
summary: Main rankings page JavaScript (754 lines). Manages season selector, ELO rankings table, predictions panel, historical simulation panel, and the Share Top-25 card download. On DOMContentLoaded it loads seasons, stats, prediction accuracy, predictions, and wires all event listeners. Calls api.js for data and date-utils.js for formatting.

name: comparison.js
summary: AP Poll vs ELO prediction comparison page (484 lines, EPIC-010). Loads season data, renders hero accuracy stats (overall ELO, AP poll, ELO advantage), breakdown stats (both-correct/elo-only/ap-only/both-wrong), regular-season vs postseason split stats, a Chart.js accuracy-over-time line chart with a postseason visual separator, and a disagreement game table.

name: matchup.js
summary: Head-to-head matchup predictor page (482 lines, EPIC-034). Loads all teams for autocomplete search, allows selecting two teams and toggling home/neutral site advantage, fetches /api/matchup to render win probability bar, side-by-side stats grid, SVG ELO history dual-line chart, head-to-head game table, and updates OG meta tags for social sharing. Supports URL param pre-fill (teamA/teamB).

name: date-utils.js
summary: Shared date utility library (140 lines) providing formatGameDate() (with optional relative date mode), getRelativeDate() (Today/Tomorrow/In X days for games within 6 days), getFullDateTime() (long-form with timezone for tooltips), isGameInPast(), and formatGameDateMobile() (numeric short format). Used across app.js, matchup.js, and team.js.

name: share.js
summary: Share and social utility module (165 lines, EPIC-036): provides copyToClipboard() with clipboard API + textarea fallback, showToast() notification, shareMatchup()/shareTeam()/sharePrediction() URL-copy helpers, and downloadTop25Card() which renders a 1200x800px Canvas image of the current Top 25 rankings (team names, ELO, record, two-column layout) and triggers a PNG download.

name: simulator.js
summary: Preseason rating simulator (EPIC-032). Loads team preseason component data (/api/preseason/components) and official weights (/api/admin/preseason-weights), provides interactive sliders for recruiting_scale, transfer_scale, returning_scale, position_scale, prev_season_weight, mean_regression, and returning_regression_scale. Re-simulates all team ratings live on slider change and renders a sortable Top 25 / All Teams table with expandable component breakdown bars. Admin mode (?admin in URL) enables saving weights back to the server.

name: team.js
summary: Team detail page JavaScript (754 lines). Loads team info, ELO/SOS stats, prediction accuracy, ELO history SVG chart, game log table, and full season schedule. Supports historical season viewing by overriding current stats with ranking history data. Handles game type filtering (regular/bowl/playoff/conference_championship) and decorates schedule rows with FCS badges, bowl/playoff/conf-champ badges, and prediction outcome cells. Updates OG meta tags for social link previews.

name: theme.js
summary: Dark/light theme toggle (IIFE, 27 lines). Applies saved theme from localStorage immediately before first paint to avoid flash. On DOMContentLoaded, wires the #theme-toggle button to switch between dark and light themes, persisting the choice to localStorage, and updates the button icon (sun/moon SVG).
